gene_description: Sortilin 1 — sorting receptor involved in hepatic lipoprotein metabolism and VLDL secretion. The 1p13.3 locus (CELSR2-PSRC1-SORT1) is one of the strongest GWAS signals for LDL cholesterol and coronary artery disease risk.

source.title: From noncoding variant to phenotype via SORT1 at the 1p13 cholesterol locus
source.finding: Variants at the 1p13.3 locus alter hepatic SORT1 expression, directly affecting VLDL secretion and LDL-C levels. The minor allele of rs629301 is associated with higher SORT1 expression, lower VLDL secretion, and reduced LDL-C and CAD risk.

GG.effect: Homozygous major allele carriers have lower hepatic SORT1 expression, leading to increased VLDL secretion and elevated LDL-C levels. Associated with increased risk of coronary artery disease, atherosclerosis, and arterial calcification. Equivalent to ~10-year additional cardiovascular risk in critical stenosis assessment.
GG.recommendation: Cardiovascular risk management: lipid-lowering diet (reduce saturated fat, increase soluble fiber, plant sterols). Consider statin therapy per clinical guidelines. Regular lipid panel monitoring. LDL-C target individualized by overall risk profile.

GT.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate SORT1 expression and LDL-C levels. Modest cardiovascular risk modification from this locus.
GT.recommendation: Heart-healthy diet. Monitor lipid levels annually. Standard cardiovascular risk assessment.

TG.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate SORT1 expression and LDL-C levels. Modest cardiovascular risk modification from this locus.
TG.recommendation: Heart-healthy diet. Monitor lipid levels annually. Standard cardiovascular risk assessment.

TT.effect: Homozygous minor allele carriers have higher hepatic SORT1 expression, resulting in reduced VLDL secretion, lower LDL-C, and reduced coronary artery disease risk. Naturally favorable lipid profile from this locus.
TT.recommendation: Standard dietary guidelines. Favorable genetic lipid profile but other risk factors still apply. Maintain healthy lifestyle.
